calllog_result.best_text: Today we want to understand how your team searches past sales and support calls.
The main issue is manual work. Our reps summarize calls in Salesforce, but product feedback stays buried in transcripts.
We need a way to search by product area, pain point, and next action.
Security review will ask about SSO, audit logs, and whether emails or phone numbers are redacted before indexing.
Next step is a pilot with 200 call transcripts and a dashboard for missing CRM fields.

transcription_summary: Sales and support call search pilot discussion with CRM gaps, security review, and missing field dashboard needs.

calllog_result.best_text: How did the pilot import go?
We found duplicate account names and missing opportunity IDs.
The transcript search is useful, but if the agent cannot connect calls to the right Salesforce account, leadership will not trust the insights.
We also want a weekly report that shows pricing objections and implementation blockers.
We will add account normalization and evidence spans for every extracted field.

transcription_summary: Pilot import follow-up covering duplicate account names, missing opportunity IDs, pricing objections, and evidence requirements.

calllog_result.best_text: Before any analysis, we need strict access controls. Some calls include account numbers and regulated information.
We need redaction, retention policy, audit logs, and a way to exclude sensitive calls.
The useful output is trend analysis by customer journey stage.
We care about onboarding friction, mobile app issues, and escalation themes.
We can start with metadata-only clustering, then allow approved reviewers to inspect evidence snippets.

transcription_summary: Inbound security and analytics discussion covering access control, redaction, retention, audit logs, and journey-stage trend analysis.

calllog_result.best_text: The first cluster report was good, but it mixed mobile login bugs with branch onboarding questions.
We need more precise product area labels and separation between support incidents and feature requests.
Also add urgency because regulatory escalations should not be buried with routine feedback.
A field for escalation reason would help routing.

transcription_summary: Cluster report feedback requesting more precise product area labels, urgency, and escalation reason fields.

calllog_result.best_text: We have hundreds of founder-led sales calls and no clean CRM hygiene.
HubSpot notes are inconsistent.
We want the agent to detect competitor mentions, pricing objections, integrations requested, and whether the prospect asked for a security review.
Would you use this for follow-up drafting too?
Yes, but only if the next action and owner are extracted reliably.

transcription_summary: Sales enrichment discussion about CRM hygiene, competitor mentions, pricing objections, integrations, security review, and next action extraction.

calllog_result.best_text: The urgent need is pipeline review.
We need to know which prospects mentioned Slack integration or Jira integration and whether pricing blocked the deal.
For existing customers, the same enrichment should identify adoption risk and expansion signals.
If the agent learns from corrections, we can review ten calls per week and improve the schema gradually.

transcription_summary: Pipeline review discussion about integration requests, pricing blockers, adoption risk, expansion signals, and correction loops.

calllog_result.best_text: Call volume spikes after promotions.
We need a searchable call archive that groups complaints by shipping delay, coupon confusion, and refund policy.
The warehouse has order metadata in BigQuery, but transcripts are separate.
The enrichment plan should link call topics to order status and campaign ID.
Please avoid exposing customer addresses in summaries.

transcription_summary: Retail support call archive discussion covering promotion spikes, complaint grouping, BigQuery metadata, campaign ID, and privacy constraints.

calllog_result.best_text: We want to compare campaigns by complaint themes.
Last week's campaign generated many calls about coupon codes not applying.
A data gap is that agents mention campaign names inconsistently.
We need campaign normalization, product category, refund risk, and sentiment.
A dashboard with evidence snippets is enough; do not show raw transcripts to broad users.

transcription_summary: Marketing analytics discussion about campaign comparison, coupon complaints, campaign normalization, refund risk, sentiment, and evidence snippets.
